title: An Old Song.
content:
The wild duck fly over
            From river to river
        And so the young lover
            Goes roving for ever.

        They fly together,
            He walks alone:
        No maiden can tether
            Him with her moan.

        At the bursting of blossom
            On her breast his head;
        He has left her bosom
            Ere the apples are red.

        Across the valley,
            Singing he goes.
        In highway and alley
            He seeks a new rose.

        Tell me, O maidens,
            You who all day
        In lyrical cadence
            Dance and play,

        Why do you proffer
            Your sweets to one,
        Who takes all you offer
            And leaves you to moan?
meaning:
This evocative piece by Edward Shanks, titled "An Old Song.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
